report_name: Ryuk Ransomware Operator Pleads Guilty to $15M Extortion Scheme

threat_actors.name: Ryuk Conspiracy Operators (2019-2020)
threat_actors.description: A conspiracy of threat actors, including Vardanyan, who deployed Ryuk ransomware between 2019 and 2020 to extort over $15 million from U.S. organizations in 'big game hunting' campaigns.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of large enterprises through ransomware deployment.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Ryuk ransomware was used to encrypt files on hundreds of servers and workstations, appending the .RYK extension.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used compromised valid accounts, including domain administrator credentials, for lateral movement and payload deployment.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Ryuk and its precursor malware often attempted to disable security software on victim networks to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers frequently deleted volume shadow copies using commands like 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to prevent easy restoration of encrypted files.

attack_patterns.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: The operators used tools like PsExec to move laterally and deploy the Ryuk ransomware across the network via SMB/Windows Admin Shares.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Initial access was often achieved via phishing campaigns that delivered first-stage malware like Emotet or TrickBot.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Trust Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The operators used tools like BloodHound to map the internal network and identify critical assets like domain controllers.

malware.name: Ryuk
malware.description: A notorious ransomware family used to encrypt hundreds of servers and workstations in targeted 'big game hunting' campaigns, demanding large ransoms in Bitcoin. It appends the .RYK extension to files and drops a ransom note named RyukReadMe.txt.

malware.name: TrickBot
malware.description: A first-stage malware dropper, often delivered via phishing, used to gain initial access to victim networks before the deployment of Ryuk.

malware.name: BazarLoader
malware.description: A malware dropper, mentioned as a common precursor to Ryuk, used to gain initial access to victim networks.

malware.name: Emotet
malware.description: A first-stage malware dropper, often delivered via phishing, used to gain initial access to victim networks before the deployment of Ryuk.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Used by the operators for reconnaissance and lateral movement within the victim's network after initial access.

tools.name: BloodHound
tools.description: Used by the operators to map the internal network, identify critical assets, and escalate privileges.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Used by the attackers to push the Ryuk ransomware executable to hundreds of servers and workstations simultaneously after gaining domain administrator credentials.

identities.name: Vardanyan
identities.description: A foreign national who pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court for his role in the Ryuk ransomware conspiracy.

identities.name: U.S. Companies
identities.description: A class of victims targeted by the Ryuk ransomware conspiracy, including specific companies in Michigan, Oregon, and Texas.

identities.name: U.S. School District
identities.description: A school district targeted by the Ryuk ransomware conspiracy, highlighting the disruption to essential public services.

indicators.name: Ryuk Ransomware Note File Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Ryuk Ransomware Note File Creation
id: 1e9a8b7c-6d5e-4f3a-2b1c-0d9e8f7a6b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the Ryuk ransom note 'RyukReadMe.txt'.
references:
  - https://dailyhodl.com/2026/07/13/foreign-national-admits-guilt-in-15000000-bitcoin-ransomware-attacks-on-us-firms
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\RyukReadMe.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the Ryuk ransom note file, 'RyukReadMe.txt', which is dropped in each directory where files are encrypted.

indicators.name: Ryuk Encrypted File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Ryuk Encrypted File Extension
id: 2f8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects files being created or renamed with the .RYK extension, characteristic of Ryuk ransomware.
references:
  - https://dailyhodl.com/2026/07/13/foreign-national-admits-guilt-in-15000000-bitcoin-ransomware-attacks-on-us-firms
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.RYK'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the `.RYK` extension, which is the core indicator of Ryuk encryption activity.

indicators.name: Ryuk Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via vssadmin
id: 3a7c6d5e-4f3a-4b2c-0d9e-8f7a6b5c4d3e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://dailyhodl.com/2026/07/13/foreign-national-admits-guilt-in-15000000-bitcoin-ransomware-attacks-on-us-firms
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used by Ryuk operators to delete Volume Shadow Copies, preventing system recovery from local backups.

indicators.name: Potential PsExec Execution for Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec Execution
id: 4b6d5e4f-3a2b-4c1d-9e8f-7a6b5c4d3e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, which can be used by attackers for lateral movement and remote execution, as seen in Ryuk campaigns.
references:
  - https://dailyhodl.com/2026/07/13/foreign-national-admits-guilt-in-15000000-bitcoin-ransomware-attacks-on-us-firms
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.t1570
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a tool commonly used by Ryuk operators to remotely deploy the ransomware binary on multiple systems.

relationships.source_name: Potential PsExec Execution for Lateral Movement
